topic: Two strangers realize they've been dating the same person
raw_output:
[story 1]

The lukewarm coffee in my hand suddenly felt like sandpaper against my teeth. I stared at the man across the small cafe table, a familiar set of slightly rumpled linen shirt sleeves and the way he habitually tucked a stray curl behind his ear. My jaw tightened, a subtle clenching I quickly tried to mask by focusing intently on the pattern of the chipped ceramic mug. Then, he looked up, catching my gaze, and offered that easy, slightly lopsided smile. A wave of sheer exasperation washed over me. *Seriously?* I mentally sighed, the sound trapped somewhere in my chest. I shifted in my seat, angling my body away, hoping the proximity would somehow dissipate the strange, unwelcome recognition blooming in my gut.

[story 2]

"Excuse me, is this seat taken?" The question felt brittle on my tongue. I glanced over, and there he was: the same slightly too-loud laugh, the exact shade of hazel in his eyes. My shoulders tensed almost imperceptibly beneath my sweater. I took a slow, deliberate sip of my water, letting the silence stretch until it became taut. When he finally approached, his expression shifting into that familiar, overly earnest concern, I just offered a clipped, "No," my voice flatter than usual. I resisted the urge to physically lean back further, focusing instead on the microscopic dust motes dancing in the afternoon light, trying to keep my features utterly neutral.

[story 3]

I watched him across the crowded bookstore aisle, his silhouette instantly recognizable against the backdrop of towering paperbacks. A sharp, internal clench tightened my stomach. It was impossible. I’d been trying to ignore the sudden, unwelcome spike of irritation, but every time his shadow fell across my path, my posture became rigidly defensive. When he finally turned, his eyes met mine, and for a split second, the world seemed to tilt slightly off its axis. I quickly looked down at the spine of a novel, my fingers gripping the cover so tightly my knuckles went white. I took a deep, shaky breath, forcing a polite, almost vacant expression onto my face, trying to project an aura of complete disinterest.
